subject: Re: can WARP remove gold fiducials?
body:
Hi Lorenzo,

wow!   Thanks so much!  I was about to use imod's find beads 3D, then 
subtract from the raw frames, then reimport into WARP.  Seems like a pain.

We are using the latest version of WARP though.  Would it work to also 
install ver 1.0.9 then load the current session into there, do the gold 
processing, then close 1.0.9 and continue in 1.1?   Otherwise I will just 
try to retrain using the steps below? 

http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=137

thanks again, I really appreciate it

Jesse

On Wednesday, October 25, 2023 at 10:32:54 AM UTC+2 Warp wrote:

> Hey Jesse,
>
> We have a nice little guide over at 
> https://teamtomo.org/walkthroughs/EMPIAR-10164/2d-preprocessing.html 
> where you can find some info about this. If you switch to the `Fiducials` 
> tab in the text, you'll see how to use GoldNet to mask out fiducials in 
> reconstructions.
>
> Note that this works with 1.0.9, not 1.1, for which you'd have to retrain 
> the network.
>
> Best,
> Lorenzo
>
> On Wed, Oct 25, 2023 at 10:29 AM mrJ <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> hi all,
>>
>> Just wondering if there is any way to remove gold fiducials in WARP?  
>>
>> Currently the gold is disrupting template matching in the tomograms.
>>
>> thanks!
>>
>> Jesse
>>
>> --
